info.title: ArgaamPlus API
info.description: RESTful v1.1 endpoints (/api/v1.1/...). New, market-ready surface.

post.summary: Issue an access token from username + password (general scope).
post.description:
Returns an `access_token` and `refresh_token`. The default
scope grants read access to public v1.1 endpoints.
            
### Example
```
POST /api/v1.1/auth/token
Content-Type: application/json
            
{ "username": "john@example.com", "password": "secret" }
```
            
### Response
```json
{
  "accessToken": "eyJhbGciOi...",
  "refreshToken": "eyJ0eXAiOi...",
  "expiresIn": 3600
}
```

post.summary: Mint a new access token from a refresh token.
post.description:
Use when the access token has expired. The refresh token is
valid until logout or its explicit expiry (~30 days).
            
### Example
```
POST /api/v1.1/auth/refresh
Content-Type: application/json
            
{ "refreshToken": "eyJ0eXAiOi..." }
```

get.summary: Get the SignalR channel mapping for the current connection.
get.description:
Internal helper used by the realtime price stream client
to discover its SignalR channel and active connections.

post.description:
Restricts the JWT audience to the Charts API. Use this from
charting widgets that should not have access to other
surfaces.
            
### Example
```
POST /api/v1.1/auth/charts/token
Content-Type: application/json
            
{ "username": "...", "password": "...", "applicationId": "..." }
```
Restricts the JWT to the Briefing service (`/api/v1.1/briefing/*`).
Restricts the JWT to the Strategy/Argaam-100 surface.
Restricts the JWT to the macroeconomic data surface.

post.summary: Sign a single device session out.
post.description:
Invalidates the bearer token associated with the supplied
`uniqueId` (typically the device or session id encoded in
the JWT).
            
### Example
```
POST /api/v1.1/auth/logout?uniqueId=device-abc123
Authorization: Bearer eyJ0eXAi...
```

get.summary: Country -> markets -> sectors -> companies tree.
get.description:
Returns the full discovery tree for selecting an entity to
chart. Common use: populate the cascade dropdowns in the
charting tool.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/charts/universe?countryId=1
```

get.summary: Universe filtered to a single stock symbol.
get.description:
Convenience variant of `/universe` that pre-selects a
company. Useful when deep-linking to a specific stock
chart from outside the platform.

get.summary: Companies grouped by sector across the GCC.
get.description:
Cross-market lookup useful for **GCC-wide peer analysis**.
Returns one entry per sector with the listed companies inside.

get.summary: All companies trading on a single market.
Suggested peer companies for a target.
get.description:
Argaam picks peers using a blend of sector membership,
market-cap proximity, and editorial curation. Useful as the
default selection on a peer-comparison chart.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/charts/companies/2222/peers?marketId=1
```

get.summary: Country/market flag metadata for a company.
get.description:
Returns the country flag image identifiers for company-level
chart annotations.

get.summary: Company ticker bar (price + change for chart headers).
get.description:
Lightweight payload designed for the **chart title strip**
and floating ticker badges.

get.summary: Latest price for charting axes/markers.
Stock-summary numbers (52w hi/lo, vol, mcap, etc.).
get.description:
Aggregate of the most-used stock metrics. Pair with a price
chart to show stat boxes alongside the visualization.

get.summary: Recent stock-price change deltas (D, W, M, YTD, 1Y).
get.description:
Returns standard percentage changes used in performance
summary cards.

get.summary: Analyst recommendation timeline (buy/hold/sell history).
get.description:
Returns recommendations from covering analysts as a time
series, ideal for an analyst-history chart layered above
the price line.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/charts/companies/2222/analyst-recommendation?marketId=1&from=2023-01-01&to=2023-12-31
```

get.summary: Major shareholders rendered for the ownership chart.
Foreign ownership history (year-by-year).
get.description:
Used to chart the trend of foreign ownership through CMA
quotas opening up over time.

get.summary: Macro charts ranking data (e.g., GDP growth).
get.description:
Underlying dataset for macro-economic ranking visualizations.
Use for cross-country charts when comparing macro indicators
like GDP, inflation, FX, etc.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/charts/macro/ranking?column=gdpGrowth&marketId=1
```

get.summary: List all tracked commodities.
get.description:
Returns the full catalogue Argaam tracks: codes, names, units,
reference exchanges, and category (energy, metals, agri, etc.).
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/commodities
```

get.summary: Latest quotes for "regular" actively-traded commodities.
get.description:
Refreshed continuously during market hours. Use this endpoint
for **trading screens** and **price tickers**.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/commodities/quotes/regular
```

get.summary: Historical quotes for a "regular" commodity.
get.description:
Returns end-of-day archive prices for the supplied commodity
across the requested date window.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/commodities/1/quotes/regular/history?from=2024-01-01&to=2024-01-31
```

get.summary: Shipping rates / freight prices for a commodity.
get.description:
Returns chartered / spot shipping prices. Useful for
landed-cost analysis when modeling regional trade flows.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/commodities/1/shipping?from=2024-01-01&to=2024-01-31
```

parameters.name: metric

get.summary:
Get the lowest 20 companies in a market for a given metric (P/E or P/B).
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/company/lowest-20/{marketID}/price-earnings
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/company/lowest-20/{marketID}/price-per-book

Get top-rank-20 companies by a key metric across multiple years.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/company/get-top-rank-20-companies/{lang}/{key}/{numberOfYear}/{companyCount}

Search and list companies (paged).
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/company/get-companies/{LanguageId}

Get a company by its trading symbol.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/company/get-profile-by-symbol/{Symbol}

Get major shareholders for a company by ID or symbol.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/company/get-major-shareholders-by-Company

Get foreign investor ownership trend for a company by symbol.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/company/foreign-investor-ownership-by-company

Get corporate actions (capital changes, dividends, splits, etc.) for a symbol.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/company/corporate-actions/{Symbol}

Get aggregated financial statement values for one company across years.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/company/get-company-financial-statement/{companyid}/{fromYear}/{toYear}/{fiscalPeriodType}/{fieldid}

Stock classification ratios (cross-company).
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/company/stock-classification-get-ratios

Market-level ratios across companies.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/company/get-market-ratios/{fiscalPeriodTypeID}/{marketId}/{excludeAramco}
Catalogue of all tracked consumer goods (materials).
get.description:
Returns the full taxonomy: material id, name (Ar/En),
category (food, beverages, building, fuel, etc.), unit, and
reference region.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/consumer-goods
```

get.summary: Latest price for a specific material.
get.description:
Single-material variant of `/prices`. Returns the most
recent quote plus daily change.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/consumer-goods/42/prices
```

get.summary: Historical prices for a specific material.
get.description:
Time series for trend analysis. Returns end-of-day archive
prices over the requested window.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/consumer-goods/42/prices/history?from=2023-01-01&to=2023-12-31
```

post.description:
Creates (or de-duplicates against) a Salesforce Lead record.
Returns the Salesforce record id and assignment metadata.
            
### Example
```
POST /api/v1.1/crm/leads
Content-Type: application/json
            
{
  "firstName": "John",
  "lastName": "Doe",
  "email": "john@example.com",
  "phone": "+9665...",
  "company": "Acme Corp",
  "source": "pricing-page",
  "useCase": "research-platform"
}
```

get.summary: Financial statement for a single company across years.
get.description:
Returns the requested statement values (income / balance /
cash flow) for one company across the requested year range.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/excel/companies/2222/financials?fromYear=2020&toYear=2024&fiscalPeriodType=1&lang=2&fields=20,54,182
```

get.summary: Compare latest-only financial statement values across companies.
get.description:
Same shape as `/compare/financials` but returns only the
most recent period per company. Faster when you only need
the snapshot.

get.summary: Compare financial ratios across multiple companies.
get.description:
Same shape as `/compare/financials` but for **financial
ratios** (P/E, P/B, ROE, etc.). Pass ratio IDs in `frIds`.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/excel/companies/compare/ratios?companyIds=2222,2280&fromYear=2020&toYear=2024&fiscalPeriodType=1&lang=2&frIds=1,2,3
```

get.summary: Compare latest-only financial ratios across companies.
Compare latest-only attribute values across companies.
The full Excel add-in field metadata (taxonomy).
get.description:
Returns the schema used by the Excel add-in to render its
dropdowns: statement types, fields per statement, ratios,
attributes, and reference enums. Cache this on first launch
of the add-in and refresh weekly.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/excel/add-in/metadata
```

get.summary: Excel add-in i18n / configuration bundle.
get.description: Bundle of language-specific labels and add-in defaults.

post.summary: Export a filtered universe of companies as CSV/JSON.
post.description:
Submits an asynchronous job that renders a flat record per
matching company. Use `fields` to project only the columns you
need; pass `["all"]` for the full schema.
            
### Example request
```
POST /api/v1.1/export/companies/csv
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer eyJ...
            
{
  "marketId": 1,
  "sectorId": 28,
  "format": "csv",
  "fields": ["companyId", "ticker", "marketCap", "peRatio"],
  "compressed": false
}
```
            
### Example response
```json
{ "jobId": "exp_abc123", "status": "queued", "createdAt": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z" }
```
            
Poll `GET /jobs/{jobId}` until `status = completed`.

Produces a multi-company financial dataset (income-statement,
balance-sheet, or cash-flow) spanning the requested number of
historical years.
            
### Example request
```
POST /api/v1.1/export/financials
            
{
  "companyIds": [2222, 2280, 1140],
  "statement": "income-statement",
  "period": "annual",
  "years": 10,
  "format": "xlsx"
}
```

get.summary: Export historical end-of-day market data.
get.description:
Returns end-of-day market-level data (index, breadth, volume,
value) for the requested date window.
            
### Example request
```
GET /api/v1.1/export/markets/1?from=2020-01-01&to=2024-12-31&format=csv
```

post.summary: Export the result set of a saved screener query.
post.description:
Server-side variant of `/api/v1.1/screener/run`. The query is
executed and the result is delivered as the chosen format.
            
### Example request
```
POST /api/v1.1/export/screener
            
{
  "criteria": { "marketId": 1, "peRatio_max": 15, "dividendYield_min": 3 },
  "format": "xlsx",
  "includeCharts": true
}
```

The most flexible variant. Pass entity types, per-entity
filters, per-entity field projections, and an output format.
            
### Example request
```
POST /api/v1.1/export/custom
            
{
  "entities": ["companies", "sectors"],
  "filters": { "marketId": 1 },
  "fields": {
    "companies": ["companyId", "ticker", "marketCap"],
    "sectors":   ["sectorId", "nameEn", "marketCap"]
  },
  "format": "xlsx",
  "compressed": true
}
```

get.summary: List all export jobs for the authenticated account.
get.description:
Returns jobs in descending order of creation time. Completed
jobs include a `downloadUrl` pointing to the download endpoint.
            
### Example response
```json
[
  {
    "jobId": "exp_abc123",
    "status": "completed",
    "createdAt": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z",
    "completedAt": "2024-01-15T10:00:05Z",
    "downloadUrl": "/api/v1.1/export/jobs/exp_abc123/download",
    "sizeBytes": 14238
  }
]
```

get.summary: Get the current status of a single export job.
get.description:
Poll this endpoint until `status` reaches `completed` or `failed`.
Alternatively subscribe to the `export.completed` webhook event
to avoid polling.
            
| Status      | Description                                    |
|-------------|------------------------------------------------|
| `queued`    | Waiting in the worker queue.                   |
| `running`   | Currently being rendered.                      |
| `completed` | Payload ready — use `downloadUrl`.             |
| `failed`    | Rendering error — see `error` field.           |

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: The job ID returned by the submission endpoint.

get.summary: Download the completed export file.
get.description:
Streams the rendered file. The `Content-Disposition` header
carries a suggested filename (`export_{jobId}.csv` / `.json`).
            
Returns **409 Conflict** when the job is not yet `completed`.
Returns **404 Not Found** when the payload has been evicted
from memory — re-submit the job to regenerate it.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: The job ID returned by the submission endpoint.

post.summary: Upload an image via the legacy `Image` payload.
post.description:
Existing-client compatibility endpoint. Prefer
`/upload/base64` or `/upload/cdn` for new integrations.

post.summary: Upload a chat attachment (used by mobile streaming chat).
post.description:
Optimized for small inline attachments in real-time chat
surfaces. Returns a CDN URL the chat client embeds in the
message envelope.

get.summary: List all fund managers.
get.description:
Returns the catalogue of fund-management firms tracked by
Argaam: manager ID, name (Ar/En), licensing jurisdiction,
and fund counts.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/funds/managers
```

get.summary: Detailed information for a specific fund.
get.description:
Full fund record: investment objective, fees, benchmark,
distribution policy, latest NAV, and reference dates.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/funds/managers/12/funds/345
```

get.summary: Latest NAV / unit values for a fund.
get.description:
Returns the most recent observed NAV values plus daily,
weekly, and monthly change percentages.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/funds/managers/12/funds/345/values
```

get.summary: Historical NAV values for a fund over a date range.
get.description:
End-of-day NAV archive used to compute fund performance
(1M, 3M, 1Y, since-inception) and benchmark deltas.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/funds/managers/12/funds/345/values/history?from=2023-01-01&to=2023-12-31
```

get.summary: List all indicator groups.
get.description:
Returns the catalogue of groups (e.g., Saudi GDP, UAE
inflation, GCC monetary aggregates). Each entry includes
id, name (Ar/En), country, frequency, and field count.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/indicators/groups
```

get.summary: List markets available for the Low-PE strategy.
get.description:
Returns the curated subset of markets the strategy supports
(typically: Saudi, Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Qatar, Kuwait, Bahrain,
Oman). Differs from `/markets` in that internationally
listed markets are excluded.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/investment-strategy/markets
```

get.summary: List IPOs in a market.
get.description:
Returns the IPO calendar for the requested market: upcoming,
active subscription, and recently listed offerings.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/ipos/markets/1
```

Stock fundamentals widget. Legacy: /ir-widget/stock-info
Major shareholders widget. Legacy: /ir-widget/major-shareholders
Company milestones (key developments). Legacy: /ir-widget/milestones
Subsidiaries widget. Legacy: /ir-widget/subsidiaries
Shareholder history. Legacy: /ir-widget/shareholders-history
Foreign ownership ratio. Legacy: /ir-widget/foreign-ownerships
Foreign ownership detail table. Legacy: /ir-widget/foreign-investor-ownership
Company ticker widget. Legacy: /ir-widget/company-ticker
Board / management individuals. Legacy: /ir-widget/company-individuals
Yearly executive salaries. Legacy: /ir-widget/company-salaries
Chart data table for IR price chart. Legacy: /ir-widget/chart-data-table/{fromDate?}/{toDate?}
Annual / quarterly financial results widget. Legacy: /ir-widget/financial-results
Projects and news widget. Legacy: /ir-widget/projects-news
Capital change widget. Legacy: /ir-widget/capital-change
Recent dividends widget. Legacy: /ir-widget/recent-dividends
Capital change history. Legacy: /ir-widget/capital-change-history
Capital + dividend history. Legacy: /ir-widget/capital-dividend-history
Capital chart data. Legacy: /ir-widget/capital-chart-data
Dividend chart data. Legacy: /ir-widget/dividend-chart-data
Dividend per share chart data. Legacy: /ir-widget/dividend-per-share-chart-data
Detailed financial ratios widget. Legacy: /ir-widget/financial-ratios
Press releases. Legacy: /ir-widget/press-releases/{lang?}
Board of Directors holdings. Legacy: /ir-widget/bod-holdings
Other report types. Legacy: /ir-widget/Company-Other-Report-Types
Investors presentations. Legacy: /ir-widget/investors-presentations
Business segments. Legacy: /ir-widget/business-segments
Geographic segments. Legacy: /ir-widget/geolocation-segments
Company reports list. Legacy: /ir-widget/company-reports/{lang?}
Company reports + presentations combined. Legacy: /ir-widget/company-reports-presentations

Investment calculator (by amount).
Legacy: /ir-widget/investment-calculator-by-amount/{amount}/{investmentDate}/{endsDate}/{reInvest}

Investment calculator (by number of shares).
Legacy: /ir-widget/investment-calculator-by-shares/{numberOfShares}/{investmentDate}/{endsDate}/{reInvest}
Analyst estimates widget. Legacy: /ir-widget/analyst-estimates
Analyst opinions widget. Legacy: /ir-widget/analyst-opinions
Negotiated deals widget. Legacy: /ir-widget/negotiated-deals
Mergers and acquisitions widget. Legacy: /ir-widget/mergers-acquisitions

Aggregated overview widget (price, news, ratios, events bundled).
Legacy: /ir-widget/overview/{lang?}
Earnings records. Legacy: /ir-widget/get-earnings/{lang?}
Article certifications. Legacy: /ir-widget/get-article-certification
List newsletters the user can subscribe to.
get.description:
Returns the active newsletter catalog from SFMC. Each entry
includes id, display name (Ar/En), description, and the
publishing cadence.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/marketing/newsletters
```

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: SFMC API key. Defaults to the platform default.

get.summary:
List all markets (domestic + international).
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/markets/get-markets and get-international-markets

Get current market data for a specific market.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/markets/get-markets-data/{marketID}

Get historical (archive) market data.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/markets/get-markets-data/{marketID}/archive

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Archive start date (yyyy-MM-dd). Defaults to yesterday.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Archive end date (yyyy-MM-dd). Defaults to today.

get.summary:
List companies traded on a specific market.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/markets/get-market-companies/{marketID}

Get current data for an international market.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/markets/get-international-markets-data/{marketID}

Get archive data for an international market.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/markets/get-international-markets-data/{marketID}/archive

Get Shariah-compliant companies for a market.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/markets/get-shariah-compliant-companies/{marketID}

Get Shariah-compliant companies for a market filtered by certifying institution.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/markets/get-shariah-compliant-companies/{marketID}/{institutionID}

Get mergers and acquisitions for a market.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/markets/get-mergers-acquisitions/{marketID}

Get details of a specific merger/acquisition deal.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/markets/get-mergers-acquisitions-details/{mergerAcquisitionID}

Get analyst estimates aggregated across a market.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/markets/get-analyst-estimates/{marketID}

Get analyst opinions across companies in a market.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/markets/get-analyst-opinions/{marketID}

Get top movers (gainers, losers, most-active) for a market.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/markets/get-market-movers/{marketID}

Get the corporate event calendar for a market.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/markets/get-market-events/{marketID}

Get major shareholders aggregated across a market.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/markets/get-market-major-shareholders/{marketID}

Get the consolidated financial statement PDF index for a market for a given year.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/markets/get-market-financial-statement-pdf/{marketID}

Get aggregated financial results for a market.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/markets/financial-results/{marketID}

Get financial results for a specific company within a market.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/markets/financial-results/{marketID}/{companyID}

Get key market indicators (TASI, NOMU, parallel market metrics).
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/markets/get-market-indicators/{marketid}

Get sectors within a market with paging and search.
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/markets/get-market-sectors/{marketid}/{languageid}

Get the canonical stock classification list (cross-market).
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/markets/get-stock-classification-market-companies

List companies across all markets (paged).
Legacy: /api/v1.0/json/markets/get-all-markets/{languageid}
List articles published in a date range, paged.
get.description:
Returns the firehose of Argaam articles for the requested time
window. Use this endpoint for **chronological feeds** such as a
news sidebar or mobile timeline.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/news/articles?from=2024-01-01&to=2024-01-31&lang=en&page=1&pageSize=50
```
            
### Tips
- Both dates accept ISO-8601 (`2024-01-31`) or any
  M:System.DateTime.Parse(System.String)-compatible format.
- Defaults: `from = yesterday`, `to = now`, `lang = ar`,
  `pageSize = 200` (max).

get.summary: Article body in AMP-compliant HTML.
get.description:
AMP (Accelerated Mobile Pages) variant for high-performance
mobile rendering and SEO ranking.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/news/articles/12345/amp?lang=en
```

get.summary: Article details by id and language id (legacy lookup).
get.description:
Convenience overload that takes numeric `languageId` (1 = Arabic,
2 = English) instead of the `lang` string code. Provided for
backwards compatibility with v1.0 clients.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/news/articles/by-id/12345?languageId=2
```

get.summary: Articles tagged to a specific company section.
get.description:
Sections are the editorial categories Argaam maintains per
company (e.g., disclosures, analyst estimates, financial reports).
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/news/companies/2222/sections/26?lang=1&page=1&pageSize=10
```

get.summary: Articles tagged to a specific market section.
get.description:
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/news/markets/1/sections/3?lang=1&orderBy=publish-date
```

get.summary: All articles for a market section (no section pre-filter).
get.description:
Variant of `/markets/{marketId}/sections/{sectionId}` that
returns the un-filtered superset.

get.summary: Articles enriched with stock-symbol references for the period.
get.description:
Returns articles that mention listed companies, with each
article including the symbols referenced inside the body.
Use for **NLP / sentiment-tagging pipelines**.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/news/articles/with-symbols?lang=2&from=2024-01-01&to=2024-01-31&marketId=1
```

get.summary: Home-page main news feed.
get.description:
Curated headline list designed for the Argaam home page or
any landing-page widget. Optionally filter by stock symbol.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/news/home?sectionId=2&marketId=1&languageId=1&stockSymbol=2222
```

get.summary: Articles by date range with optional filters.
get.description:
Calendar-driven listing: combines arbitrary date range with
optional company / paging.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/news/articles/by-date?languageId=2&from=2024-01-01&to=2024-01-31&stockSymbol=2222&page=1&pageSize=50
```

get.summary: Available article type taxonomies.
get.description:
Article types group editorial content into broad categories
(news, analysis, press release, etc.). Use to build filter UIs.

get.summary: Article source registry (publishers, agencies).
get.description:
Returns the list of upstream sources Argaam aggregates from.
Useful when displaying article attribution.

get.summary: Company-detail bundle for the home/news landing.
get.description:
Used by the home-page article widget to load a small set of
company facts in one round trip alongside the headlines.

post.summary: Increment view/comment counters for an article.
post.description:
Best-effort counter; returns `204 No Content` on success.
Call from your client analytics layer when an article is
rendered or a comment is posted.
            
### Example
```
POST /api/v1.1/news/articles/12345/views?typeId=1
```

get.summary: Aggregated comment / view analytics for an entity.
get.description:
Returns view + comment counts. Drives reader-engagement
dashboards and trending-content rankings.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/news/analytics/comments/1/12345
```

post.summary: Subscribe a device to a notification topic.
post.description:
Topic identifiers (e.g., `earnings`, `disclosures`,
`breaking-news`) define what kinds of notifications the
user wants to receive on this device.
            
### Example
```
POST /api/v1.1/notifications/enable?notificationType=earnings&lang=ar&deviceToken=ZXh...
Authorization: Bearer eyJ...
```

post.summary: Unsubscribe a device from a notification topic.
post.description:
Inverse of `/enable`. Removes the SNS topic subscription so
the device stops receiving notifications of that type.
            
### Example
```
POST /api/v1.1/notifications/disable?resultType=json&notificationType=earnings&lang=ar&deviceToken=ZXh...
Authorization: Bearer eyJ...
```

get.summary: Latest value of the Argaam Petrochemical Price Index.
get.description:
Returns the current index level plus daily / weekly / monthly
changes. Drop into a header ticker on a sector dashboard.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/petrochemicals/index
```

get.summary: Historical values of the Argaam Petrochemical Price Index.
get.description:
Daily index history for the supplied window. Use for trend
charts and rolling-window analytics.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/petrochemicals/index/history?from=2023-01-01&to=2023-12-31
```

get.summary: Latest prices for a petrochemical product (rolling window).
get.description:
Returns prices observed during the requested rolling period
(`1D`, `1W`, `1M`, `3M`, `1Y`, `MAX`).
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/petrochemicals/products/12/prices?regionId=1&period=1M
```

get.summary: Historical prices for a petrochemical product (custom date range).
get.description:
Like `/products/{id}/prices` but takes an explicit
`from`/`to` window for arbitrary historical analysis.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/petrochemicals/products/12/prices/history?regionId=1&from=2023-01-01&to=2023-12-31
```

get.summary: Active rig counts for a region as of a date.
get.description:
Rig counts are a classic **leading indicator** for upstream
oil & gas activity, and a useful sentiment proxy for
mid-stream and downstream sectors.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/petrochemicals/rig-counts?regionId=4&asOf=2024-01-15
```

get.summary: List projects in a market (paged).
get.description:
Returns active and recently completed projects in the
requested market. Useful for building **regional project
pipelines** dashboards.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/projects/by-market/1?page=1&pageSize=20
```

get.summary: List projects involving a specific company (paged).
get.description:
Returns projects where the company appears as **client,
contractor, or consultant**. Useful for backlog assessment
in equity research.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/projects/by-company/2222?page=1&pageSize=20
```

get.summary: Detailed information for a specific project.
get.description:
Returns the complete project record: scope, parties involved,
budget breakdown, timeline, and milestones.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/projects/45678
```

get.summary: Schema describing the screener UI (sections, pages, groups).
get.description:
Returns the **complete screening schema** that powers the
Argaam web screener. Use this endpoint as the source of truth
for which fields are available, how they are grouped into
pages/sections, and the default UI layout.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/screener/structure
```
            
### Response shape
Hierarchical: `pages -> sections -> field groups -> fields`.
Each field carries display labels (Ar/En), data type, units,
allowed operators, and grouping metadata.

get.summary: Per-field configuration (formats, units, allowed operators).
get.description:
Lighter-weight alternative to `/structure`: returns just
the field-level metadata without page/section grouping.
Useful when building a custom UI or generating screener
queries programmatically.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/screener/fields/config
```

get.summary: Companies grouped by country, market, and sector.
get.description:
Convenience lookup that returns the **full universe** of listed
companies organised under their country -> market -> sector
path. Often used as the seed dataset for cross-market peer
analysis.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/screener/universe?countryId=1
```

get.summary: List all sectors in a market (default classification).
get.description:
Returns the sectors traded on the requested market. Optionally
filter to a single sector by passing `sectorId`.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/sectors/by-market/1
GET /api/v1.1/sectors/by-market/1?sectorId=28
```

parameters.name: sectorId
parameters.description: Optional sector ID for a single-record lookup.

get.summary: List Argaam proprietary sectors for a market (paged + searchable).
get.description:
Use the **Argaam taxonomy** when GICS is too coarse for the
regional industries you cover (e.g., petrochemicals broken
down into specific product families).
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/sectors/argaam?marketId=1&languageId=2&page=1&pageSize=20
```

get.summary: Latest data (price index, volume, breadth) for sector(s) in a market.
get.description:
Returns real-time sector-level performance metrics. Pair
with `/by-market/{id}` to enrich a sector dashboard.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/sectors/data/1
GET /api/v1.1/sectors/data/1?sectorId=28
```

parameters.name: sectorId
parameters.description: Optional sector ID for a single-record query.

get.summary: Historical sector data over a date range.
get.description:
End-of-day archive of sector-level metrics. Use to build
**sector rotation** charts and momentum indicators.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/sectors/data/1/history?from=2023-01-01&to=2023-12-31
GET /api/v1.1/sectors/data/1/history?sectorId=28&from=2023-01-01&to=2023-12-31
```

get.summary: List all available investment strategies.
get.description:
Returns the catalogue of strategies the platform currently
supports. Each entry includes display name (Ar/En), strategy
id, and supported markets.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/strategy
```

get.summary: Strategy widget metadata for a public IR page.
get.description:
Used by IR microsites to render the right strategy widget on
each page. Resolves widget by `pageUrl` plus `strategyId` and
optionally `companyId`/`userId` for personalized views.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/strategy/42/widget?pageUrl=/ar/company/2222&companyId=2222
```

get.summary: Argaam 100 widget data for the supplied parameter blob.
get.description:
Returns the **Argaam 100** ranked list shaped for the
embeddable widget. The `param` segment encodes filtering /
sorting (market id, sector, period) using the format
established by the V10 implementation.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/strategy/argaam-100/widget/marketId=1;sortBy=score;limit=100
```

get.summary: Argaam 100 ranked by net-profit growth.
get.description:
Returns Argaam-100 companies ordered by **net-profit CAGR**
over the horizon encoded in the `param` segment.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/strategy/argaam-100/top-growth/profit/marketId=1;years=5
```

get.summary: Argaam 100 ranked by revenue growth.
get.description:
Same shape as `/argaam-100/top-growth/profit` but ranked
by **revenue CAGR**.

get.summary: Per-company strategy metrics (cached).
get.description:
Returns the metric set the strategy uses to rank a company
(growth rates, profitability, valuation). Useful for
drill-downs from a strategy result list.

get.summary: Net-profit-per-share growth series.
get.description:
Time series of EPS growth used to render the company growth
trajectory chart inside the strategy widget.

get.summary: Aggregated company growth metrics for the strategy ranking page.
get.description:
Combines revenue + net-profit + cash-flow growth over the
configured horizon into a single response. Cached server
side; pass `cachedTime` to override.

get.summary: Aggregated API usage statistics for a date range.
get.description:
Daily usage breakdown by endpoint plus totals. Use to drive
internal usage dashboards and reconcile with billing.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/usage/statistics?from=2024-01-01&to=2024-01-31
```
            
### Response (when GA)
```json
{
  "period": { "from": "2024-01-01", "to": "2024-01-31" },
  "totalRequests": 45230,
  "successfulRequests": 44890,
  "failedRequests": 340,
  "averageResponseTimeMs": 125,
  "byEndpoint": [
    { "endpoint": "/api/v1.1/companies/{id}/profile", "requests": 12500 }
  ]
}
```

post.description:
Creates the user record. The caller must then call
`GET /verify-email?code=...` to confirm ownership of the
supplied email address before the account is fully active.
            
### Example
```
POST /api/v1.1/users
Content-Type: application/json
            
{
  "email": "john@example.com",
  "password": "secret",
  "firstName": "John",
  "lastName": "Doe",
  "mobileNo": "+9665..."
}
```

get.summary: List users (paged, admin only).
get.description:
Returns a paged list of users. Requires an admin or
platform-level token.

post.description:
Send the email + SMS codes the user received. Both must
match the values associated with `RegisterVerificationId`
for the call to succeed.
            
### Example
```
POST /api/v1.1/users/otp/verify?emailVerificationCode=123456&smsVerificationCode=654321&registerVerificationId=789
```

post.summary: Request an OTP for the Argaam Briefing app (single-channel).
post.description:
Lighter OTP flow used by the Briefing service. Email-only,
no SMS.

Used when the original verification email was lost or
expired.

get.summary: Check whether a mobile number is already registered.
get.description:
Fast pre-check for sign-up forms. Returns a boolean.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/users/mobile-exists?mobile=%2B96651234567
```

get.summary: Resolve a user from a JWT token.
get.description:
Useful for embedded analytics / SDK environments to confirm
who the bearer token represents without doing a full token
introspection.

get.summary: List all webhook subscriptions for the calling account.
get.description:
Returns active and paused subscriptions, omitting full
secrets (only a masked prefix is returned).
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/webhooks
Authorization: Bearer eyJ...
```

get.summary: Get a specific webhook subscription.
get.description:
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/webhooks/wh_abc123
```

put.description:
PATCH-style update: only the supplied fields change. Pass
`enabled=false` to pause without deleting.
            
### Example
```
PUT /api/v1.1/webhooks/wh_abc123
Content-Type: application/json
            
{ "events": ["company.earnings"], "enabled": true }
```

delete.description:
Permanently removes the subscription. To temporarily pause,
use `PUT /{webhookId}` with `enabled=false`.

post.summary: Send a synthetic test delivery to verify endpoint reachability.
post.description:
Useful immediately after subscribing to confirm your
callback URL is reachable, returns 2xx, and handles the
HMAC signature correctly.
            
### Example
```
POST /api/v1.1/webhooks/wh_abc123/test
```

get.summary: List recent delivery attempts (success and failures).
get.description:
Default: last 100 deliveries. Use `from`/`to` and `status`
to scope.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/webhooks/wh_abc123/logs?from=2024-01-01&status=failed
```

post.description:
Useful when your endpoint had transient downtime and you
want to manually replay missed events without waiting for
the automatic retry schedule.
            
### Example
```
POST /api/v1.1/webhooks/wh_abc123/retry/dlv_xyz789
```

get.summary: Catalog of all available event types.
get.description:
Returns every event type Argaam Plus emits, with a
description and an example payload shape. Use to build
subscription UIs.
            
### Example
```
GET /api/v1.1/webhooks/events
```

totalRecords.description: Total number of records available across all pages.

hasNextPage.description: Whether there is a next page available.

hasPreviousPage.description: Whether there is a previous page available.

ArgaamPlus.Core.API.Models.V11.ApiV1Meta.description: Pagination and metadata information for collection responses.

limit.description: Total request quota for the current window.

remaining.description: Remaining requests in the current window.

reset.description: UTC timestamp when the rate limit window resets.

ArgaamPlus.Core.API.Models.V11.ApiV1Rate.description: Rate limit information returned with each response.

timestamp.description: UTC timestamp when the response was generated.

ArgaamPlus.Core.API.Models.V11.ApiV1Resp.description: Standardized successful API response wrapper for v1.0.0 endpoints.

marketId.description: Optional market filter; null = all markets.

sectorId.description: Optional sector filter; null = all sectors.

ArgaamPlus.Core.API.Models.V11.BulkCompa.description: Request body for the bulk-companies export endpoint.

createdAt.description: UTC timestamp the job was queued.

completedAt.description: UTC timestamp the job completed (or null if still running).

downloadUrl.description: Signed download URL once the job completes (valid for 24h).

ArgaamPlus.Core.API.Models.V11.BulkExpor.description: Job descriptor returned for an asynchronous bulk export.

years.description: Number of historical years to include.

ArgaamPlus.Core.API.Models.V11.BulkFinan.description: Request body for the bulk-financials export endpoint.

filters.description: Per-entity filters as a free-form key/value bag.

compressed.description: Compress the export with `zip`. Default: `true` for multi-entity.

ArgaamPlus.Core.API.Models.V11.CustomExp.description: Request body for a fully-custom multi-entity export.

webhookId.description: The webhook subscription that produced this delivery.

statusCode.description: HTTP status code returned by the subscriber.

attemptedAt.description: UTC timestamp when delivery was attempted.

responseExcerpt.description: If the subscriber returned an error body, the first 1KB.

ArgaamPlus.Core.API.Models.V11.WebhookDe.description: One row in the webhook delivery log.

description.description: Human-readable description of when the event fires.

ArgaamPlus.Core.API.Models.V11.WebhookEv.description: Catalog entry describing one webhook event type.

url.description: HTTPS URL where events are delivered.

lastDeliveredAt.description: UTC timestamp of the last successful delivery.

ArgaamPlus.Core.API.Models.V11.WebhookSu.description: Webhook subscription record returned by `GET /webhooks` and `POST /webhooks`.

url.description: HTTPS URL Argaam will POST event payloads to.

events.description: Events the subscriber wants to receive (e.g., "company.earnings", "price.alert").

companyIds.description: Optional company-id filter (only events for these companies will fire).

secret.description:
Optional caller-supplied secret. If provided, will be used to compute the
HMAC-SHA256 signature of every delivery (header `X-Argaam-Signature`).
If omitted, Argaam generates one and returns it in the response.

description.description: Optional human-readable label for the subscription.

ArgaamPlus.Core.API.Models.V11.WebhookSu.description: Request body for creating a webhook subscription.

enabled.description: Enable / disable the subscription without deleting it.

ArgaamPlus.Core.API.Models.V11.WebhookSu.description: Request body for updating an existing webhook subscription.
